Raw translation notes:
  [info] household.spouseBirthDate: Derived from spouse longevity age and year; month defaulted to June.
  [info] accounts.targetAllocation: Using single-symbol allocations (VTI / BND / CASH) per bucket so the weighted return matches the Boldin rate when combined with overridden MarketAssumptions.
  [warn] windfalls.home_sale_net: Home sale modeled as cash_non_taxable windfall in the sale year. Ignores primary-home-sale tax treatment — may over-state liquidity slightly.
  [warn] spending.split: Boldin shows one recurring number; split 55/45 essential/optional as a placeholder.
  [warn] assumptions.volatility: Boldin projects deterministically; we keep MC volatility, so success-rate deltas include MC noise.
  [info] income.windfalls.home_sale_net: Home sale excluded — comparing liquid-only on both sides against Boldin taxAllocationAllAssetsAt94.

Levered translation notes:
  [info] household.spouseBirthDate: Derived from spouse longevity age and year; month defaulted to June.
  [info] accounts.targetAllocation: Using single-symbol allocations (VTI / BND / CASH) per bucket so the weighted return matches the Boldin rate when combined with overridden MarketAssumptions.
  [warn] windfalls.home_sale_net: Home sale modeled as cash_non_taxable windfall in the sale year. Ignores primary-home-sale tax treatment — may over-state liquidity slightly.
  [warn] spending.split: Boldin shows one recurring number; split 55/45 essential/optional as a placeholder.
  [warn] assumptions.volatility: Boldin projects deterministically; we keep MC volatility, so success-rate deltas include MC noise.
  [info] rules.healthcarePremiums: Overridden from Boldin lifetime totals (~$14482/yr aggregate, medicare ~$7448/person-yr).
  [info] rules.rothConversionPolicy: Disabled to isolate tax-saving contribution (Boldin does not auto-convert).
  [info] income.windfalls.home_sale_net: Home sale excluded — comparing liquid-only on both sides against Boldin taxAllocationAllAssetsAt94.
  [info] assumptions.distribution: Overridden to match Boldin Conservative preset: 5.92% mean / 11.05% stdev on equity + bond buckets.

stdout | src/fidelity-parity.test.ts > Fidelity parity — historical-methodology triangulation > reports Fidelity target, Boldin target, and our engine result side-by-side

================================================================
Triangulation: Fidelity vs Boldin vs our engine
  Same household, same 8 accounts, ~$921-924k portfolio.
  Fidelity: 250 MC sims, historical asset-class sampling.
  Boldin: Conservative preset, 5.92%/11.05% stdev override.
  Ours: 500 MC sims, historical-approximation defaults
        (equity 9.8%, intl 8.5%, bonds 5.3%, cash 3.0%).
  SAFETY CHECK — not a parity gate.
================================================================

Metric                                  Fidelity            Boldin              Ours
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
chance of success                          95.0%             48.0%             93.0%
starting balance                        $924,122          $921,173          $933,149
ending wealth (p50 median)                   n/a           $42,624       $12,513,895
ending wealth (p10)                     $436,224               n/a          $517,045
lifetime federal taxes                       n/a          $156,032          $152,325

Translation notes:
  [info] accounts: Reusing per-account balances from initialSeedData (seed-data.json). Fidelity does not publish per-account balances.
  [info] assumptions.returns: Using historical-approximation means (equity 9.8%, intl 8.5%, bonds 5.3%, cash 3.0%). Fidelity does not publish exact values; these match common SBBI long-run points and the regime under which our historical-cohorts tests pass.
  [warn] assumptions.assetMix: Fidelity reports portfolio asset mix at domestic 65.0% / foreign 9.1% / bonds 17.4% / short-term 6.7%. Our engine's bucket-level allocations from initialSeedData should aggregate near those numbers; drift between the two would be worth a dedicated allocation audit (see BACKLOG.md "Allocation check").

Interpretation hints:
  - Success rate expected to land much closer to Fidelity than
    Boldin. Boldin is running a stress preset; Fidelity and ours
    are both using historical-style returns.
  - If our success rate is > Fidelity by > 5pp, investigate
    yield-as-income accounting, withdrawal smoothing, or Roth
    conversion aggressiveness (same residuals as the Boldin
    investigation).

================================================================
